component.name: Imboard
component.summary: Developer-focused virtual keyboard for KDE Wayland

description.p: Imboard, short for Input Method Board, is a virtual keyboard for KDE Wayland desktops. It is designed for desktop tasks where compact on-screen keyboards make developer keys awkward: symbols, arrows, function keys, lock keys, shortcuts, and custom actions.
Imboard combines a transparent alphabet board with a swipeable developer pad for numbers, symbols, navigation, function keys, shortcuts, and user-defined key actions.
Imboard uses the standard XDG Remote Desktop portal to request keyboard-only control. It does not request screen sharing, pointer control, host filesystem access, or network access.

The supported target is KDE Wayland. The current release has been validated on SteamOS Desktop Mode and Fedora KDE Wayland. Gamescope/Gaming Mode and non-KDE desktops are outside the current project scope.

screenshot.caption: Main keyboard with function-key developer pad
Transparent overlay while typing in an editor

description.p: Patch release that waits for and reconnects to a replacement desktop portal after SteamOS session transitions, and keeps system permission dialogs visible above Imboard.
Patch release that restores reliable startup after switching between Gaming Mode and Desktop Mode by safely recovering runtime state left behind by an abrupt compositor or sandbox shutdown.
Minor release adding custom pad only mode, with configurable key count, grid layout, save/cancel editing, slot ordering, and a compact one-purpose window for user-selected keys.
Minor release with refreshed README guidance and quieter QML handling for custom-key picker page creation.
Patch release that restores developer-pad page content after QML component extraction and removes remaining lint/build warnings.
Patch release that removes QML startup warnings and improves native build reliability with the Qt 6.4 toolchain.
Initial release for KDE Wayland, with physical-key-style modifier behavior for layout-matched typing and Shift-lock selection.
